titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Brian Evenson Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday June 28, 2019 at 03:05

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection documents the writings, translations, academic work, and life of Brian Evenson. The papers consist of writings, including drafts for books including Altmann's Tongue (Knopf, 1994), Din of Celestial Birds (Wordcraft of Oregon, 1997), Dark Property (Thunder's Mouth Press, 2002), The Wavering Knife: Stories (Fiction Collective, 2004), Aliens: No Exit (Dark Horse, 2008), Contagion and Other Stories (Astrophil Press, 2011), and The Open Curtain (Coffee House Press, 2016), as well as unpublished works and translations; journals; correspondence; business and academic papers; childhood papers and memorabilia; writings by others; printed material containing contributions by or mentioning Evenson; artwork and posters; audiocassettes; and digital media.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Gerald W. Cloud and Brian Evenson on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2017.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Brian Evenson Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

bioghist.head: Brian Evenson
bioghist.p: Brian Evenson (b. 1966 August 12) is an American writer of both literary fiction and popular fiction, some of the latter being published under B.K. Evenson.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 29B (digital media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 29C (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Brian Evenson Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight series: I. Writings, 1980s-2004. II. Journals, 1995-2008. III. Correspondence, 1990-2017. IV. Personal, business, and academic papers, 1986-2015. V. Juvenalia, 1979-2000. VI. Writings of others, 1995-2014. VII. Publications, 1990-2006. VIII. Drawings and posters, 1985-2014.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of writings and translations by Brian Evenson.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is organized into three subseries: Published writings, Unpublished writings, and Translations.
This series maintains the arrangement as received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of drafts, corrections, and galleys for Brian Evenson's published writings, as well as related ephemera.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This suberies consists of drafs of unpublished writings by Brian Evenson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of translations by Brian Evenson of works by others, including handwritten and annotated drafts, galleys, and other papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of Brian Evenson's personal journals and notebooks, as well as related ephemera including business cards, ticket stubs, etc.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series maintains the arrangement as received.

did.unittitle: "The Eighteenth Century… the Sublime" Academic subjects examination
Gordon Lish Class notes by Evenson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of correspondence largely relating to Brian Evenson's writing and teaching. Correspondents include publishers, teachers, academic administrators, other writers, friends, family, and organizations to which Evenson submitted writing for publication.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Paper correspondence and Email.
This series maintains the arrangement as received.

did.unittitle: A, All other Correspondents Starting with the Letter A (hitherto "Various")
Dialogue: A Journal of Mormon Thought

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of papers and ephemera documenting Brian Evenson's life and work as a writer and as an educator. Materials include flyers, files relating to events and readings, correspondence, business records, and other papers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series maintains the arrangement as received.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: "Spotlight 5 KXRK 8-8-95, Jah Wobble's Invaders of the Heart Rising Above Bedlam"

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: "The Open Curtain, Christian Asplund, composer, Brian Evenson, librettist"

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of yearbooks, Boy Scouting and sporting memorabilia, photographs, and other papers from Brian Evenson's childhood.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series maintains the arrangement as received.

did.unittitle: School papers, drawings, sports, travel papers, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of drafts, typescripts, and articles by other people sent to or collected by Brian Evenson.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series maintains the arrangement as received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of texts by or about Brian Evenson, including literary and music criticism, books, short stories, and book reviews.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of drawings done by Evenson in mid 1980s as well as posters, photographss, and ephemera from promotional events or related to Evenson's life and work.
